signals.title: GBP/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: GBP/JPY at 215.95 — G10 scorecard leader GBP (+0.762%) vs laggard JPY (-0.698%) creates a 1.460% spread, the widest in the G10 complex. BoE 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0% provides a +275bp carry premium favoring GBP, while JPY remains under pressure despite a positive CESI (+13). The bear steepener regime (+77.5bp 2s10s) typically supports carry persistence until VIX exceeds 25. Watch for BoJ verbal intervention as USD/JPY holds above 162.5.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.50 — pair remains bid despite extreme retail sentiment (90% short) and institutional put skew (25d RR at -1.24). COT LF net short -115,033 (28.4% long) confirms crowded carry positioning. IV at 7.2% vs HV30 at 2.4% creates a +4.8% VRP, signaling the options market is paying a high premium for downside protection against BoJ intervention or NFP-driven reversals. Sustained break above 162.5 targets 165.0 unless NFP slows to 110k.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5682 — COT LF net short -23,314 (21.6% long) contradicts the RBNZ hike bias priced for the 8 July meeting. Retail is 90% long, creating a rare institutional-retail alignment against the spec short. With NZD real rates at -0.83% (accommodative), a hawkish RBNZ delivery triggers a significant short-squeeze risk toward 0.5800. Watch for RBNZ hike conviction to shift from current levels.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Macro Divergence + Vol
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1388 — ECB 2.25% vs Fed 3.75% maintains a -150bp carry deficit for the Euro. MUFG targets 1.20 by 2027, but near-term pressure remains as EUR CESI (-14) reflects persistent data misses vs USD CESI (+290). IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.5% (+2.6% VRP) indicates hedging demand ahead of US NFP. EUR/USD vs DXY correlation at -0.933 (z=-1.56σ) confirms the pair is the primary vehicle for USD directionality.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6900 — extreme vol risk premium (VRP +19.0%) with IV at 25.7% vs HV30 6.7% suggests the market is pricing in a massive tail-risk event or data surprise. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry advantage to AUD, yet COT LF net long +29,035 (64.9%) shows specs are already positioned for strength. AUD/USD vs Gold correlation (+0.800, z=+1.13σ) makes the pair sensitive to commodity price action if WTI $67.9 support fails.
